summary: Stellar soundboard capture of the May ’82 Greek run with a smoking Bertha > Greatest combo and an unforgettable Playin' > Uncle John's jam.
review: The three‑night Greek Theatre stint from May 21–23, 1982 is hailed as one of the band’s high points – tight transitions, a blazing Bertha‑Greatest opener, a monster Playin’ > Uncle John’s sequence and superb Bird Song and Sugar Magnolia moments.  The sbd recording is repeatedly described as “stellar”, “insane clarity” and even a “holy grail of sound”, making the show both sonically and musically a joy to revisit.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance (standout songs, tight set flow) and technical quality (soundboard praised as crystal‑clear). Weighting 70% show + 30% recording yields a rating in the high‑4 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound “stellar”, “insane clarity” and “holy grail of sound”; no complaints about dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: Playin' in the Band > Uncle John's Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: Set one opens with high‑energy Bertha‑Greatest, flows through tight transitions and strong vocal work; set two builds on a powerful Playin' > Uncle John's jam, solid Space/Drums and a refined Black Peter that leads into an uplifting Sugar Magnolia finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised repeatedly for his inventive solos (Big River jam, Bird Song, Uncle John's) and overall guitar tone; described as “smoking” and “fresh‑young”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for his “PHILNESS!!” bass energy and a memorable duel with Jerry during the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (organ) highlighted as very active on Playin' and providing bright texture throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey receive kudos for tight timing, dynamic percussion passages and an engaging drum‑solo section.
